title: Starfish Space raises more than $100 million
summary: Starfish Space has secured over $100 million in funding to expand the production of its satellite servicing spacecraft. This investment will support the company's efforts to enhance its in-space servicing capabilities.

title: NASA’s new moon base project requires operational technology systems in space, but they are vulnerable.
summary: NASA announced a $20 billion plan to build a moon base instead of a lunar orbit space station, with new Administrator Jared Isaacman highlighting the need for operational technology systems despite vulnerabilities.

title: Nominate space industry leaders for the 2026 SpaceNews Icon Awards
summary: SpaceNews is inviting nominations for the 2026 SpaceNews Icon Awards to recognize influential leaders in the space industry. The awards aim to honor innovators, collaborators, and change-makers who have made significant contributions to the sector.

title: Earth observation operators push to deliver satellite images within minutes
summary: Earth observation operators are striving to deliver high-resolution satellite images within minutes of capture. A demonstration by Vantor showed a 30-centimeter-resolution image available just 13 minutes after a WorldView Legion satellite passed overhead.

title: Artemis 2 swings around the moon
summary: Four astronauts from the U.S. and Canada traveled the furthest from Earth during the Artemis 2 mission's lunar flyby. This marked a significant milestone in human space exploration.

title: Moog Highlights Growing Satellite Bus Capabilities with Full‑Scale METEOR Reveal at Space Symposium
summary: Moog Inc. showcased its METEOR satellite bus at the Space Symposium, highlighting advancements in satellite bus capabilities. The full-scale reveal demonstrates the company's growing role in the satellite industry.

title: FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities
summary: Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is entering Japan's defense market by partnering with a local firm to expand its small satellite capabilities beyond picosatellites. This move reflects a broader shift toward more capable spacecraft for government applications.

title: The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.
summary: A former SpaceX employee highlights the need for a software strategy alongside hardware plans for a moon base. They reflect on their experience building training programs for SpaceX's mission control team.

title: Seagate Space Signs MOU with Firefly Aerospace to Collaborate on Offshore Launch Infrastructure for Alpha
summary: Seagate Space Corporation and Firefly Aerospace have signed an MOU to collaborate on developing offshore launch infrastructure for the Alpha rocket. The partnership aims to enhance launch capabilities and support commercial space missions.

title: Military space programs confront hidden supply constraints
summary: Military space programs face hidden supply chain constraints, with critical components like optical links and valves potentially becoming bottlenecks. An industry report highlights these chokepoints as a growing concern for program sustainability.

title: Watch Comet MAPS get destroyed by cataclysmic fragmentation near the sun
summary: Comet MAPS fragmented catastrophically near the sun, ending hopes of a bright display in the evening sky. The dramatic breakup was observed as the comet approached perihelion.

title: 'Unreal' solar eclipse: Artemis 2 crew just saw one of the rarest sights in spaceflight history
summary: Artemis 2 astronauts witnessed a rare solar eclipse from beyond the Moon during their lunar flyby, marking a historic moment in spaceflight.

title: The Artemis 2 astronauts just flew by the moon. Lunar scientists can't wait for what's next
summary: The Artemis 2 astronauts completed a lunar flyby, marking a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program. Lunar scientists express excitement about the future of human lunar exploration and the technological advancements it brings.

title: Moon milestones: A rundown of Artemis 2's many spaceflight firsts
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission achieved the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years, marking multiple historic spaceflight milestones beyond this initial achievement.

title: 'This is really big stuff.' Trump hails NASA Artemis 2 astronauts after historic moon flyby feat
summary: Former US President Donald Trump praised NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts for their historic moon flyby. The mission marks a significant milestone in lunar exploration.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts fly around the moon in record-breaking lunar loop by NASA
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts completed a record-breaking lunar flyby, viewing parts of the moon never before seen by humans. The mission set a new spaceflight milestone during the flyby on April 6.

title: Moon music: Here's a Spotify playlist of the Artemis 2 crew's wakeup songs
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 crew has a custom Spotify playlist of wake-up songs played by mission control to start their day with positivity. The playlist is now available for public listening.

title: Moon memorial: Artemis 2 astronauts name lunar 'bright spot' after mission commander's late wife
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 crew named a lunar 'bright spot' after the mission commander's late wife during their record-breaking journey. The dedication was part of a personal tribute while traveling the farthest distance from Earth ever achieved by astronauts.

title: Artemis 2 breaks humanity's all-time distance record during historic loop around the moon (video)
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission has broken the all-time distance record for human spaceflight, surpassing the Apollo 13 crew's 1970 achievement. The historic loop around the moon marks a key milestone in the Artemis program.

title: 'Maul – Shadow Lord' is the darkest and most focused animated 'Star Wars' show yet
summary: The animated series 'Maul – Shadow Lord' is highlighted as the darkest and most focused 'Star Wars' show, focusing on Darth Maul. However, it faces familiar frustrations typical of a debut season.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission crew achieved a record for the farthest human spaceflight, with Orion's closest lunar approach updated to 4,067 miles. The mission also included updates to the farthest distance Orion will travel and statements about crew firsts.

title: NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview
summary: NASA's Northrop Grumman CRS-24 mission will deliver 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station using the Cygnus XL spacecraft, marking its second flight.

title: Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II
summary: The Artemis II crew reached a milestone by traveling farther than any humans in history, orbiting the far side of the Moon. The event is being broadcast live on NASA's YouTube channel and NASA+ on April 6, 2026.

title: Grants
summary: NASA has provided guidance for submitting grant status requests through an automated email system. The memo also addresses recent updates related to OMB Memorandum M-25-14 and temporary restraining orders.

title: The Near Side of the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft captured an image of the Moon's near side on April 4, 2026, highlighting ancient lava flows unique to this side. The photo was taken by the Artemis II astronauts aboard Orion.

title: Thinking of You, Earth
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission, scheduled for April 4, 2026, will see Commander Reid Wiseman and crew travel towards the Moon, with Wiseman observing Earth from the Orion spacecraft. The mission includes NASA astronauts Christina Koch and Victor Glover, as well as CSA astronaut.

title: NASA Answers Your Most Pressing Artemis II Questions
summary: NASA updated details about the Artemis II mission, including corrected timings for lunar flyby activities and a distance record. The updates were made on April 5 and 6, 2026.

title: Illuminated in Orion
summary: NASA astronaut Christina Koch and CSA astronaut Jeremy Hansen are seen inside the Orion crew capsule in a photo taken in April 2026. The image highlights the crew's activities during the mission.

title: NASA Sets Coverage for Northrop Grumman’s CRS-24 Resupply Launch
summary: NASA, Northrop Grumman, and SpaceX are targeting April 8 for the CRS-24 resupply mission to the ISS. The Cygnus XL spacecraft will carry 11,000 pounds of cargo aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket.

title: Artemis II breaks record, conducts lunar flyby
summary: Artemis II achieved a record-breaking lunar flyby with the Orion spacecraft Integrity, carrying four astronauts. The mission marks a significant milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to loft Cygnus to ISS, Minotaur IV to launch from California
summary: A Falcon 9 rocket is set to launch a Cygnus spacecraft to the ISS, while a Minotaur IV rocket is scheduled to launch from California. These launches occur as NASA's Artemis II mission progresses.

title: Orion Spacecraft Races Toward Historic Lunar Flyby in Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission is in its mid-flight phase as the Orion spacecraft approaches a historic lunar flyby. This marks a significant milestone for the mission, which aims to carry astronauts around the Moon.

title: Artemis II Orion performs TLI Burn — Now headed to the Moon
summary: NASA's Orion spacecraft successfully performed the Trans-Lunar Injection (TLI) burn, marking a historic milestone as it heads to the Moon. This achievement is part of the Artemis II mission, the first crewed lunar mission in over 50 years.

title: Artemis II returns humanity to the Moon with launch on Wednesday evening
summary: Artemis II is set to return humanity to the Moon with a launch scheduled for Wednesday evening. The mission marks a significant milestone in space exploration following the Apollo era.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz lifting off from Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: America's Rocket Factory is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights advancements in space exploration and industrial contributions to lunar missions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit from California for the Transporter 16 mission. This marks another successful rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace aborted the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The European private space launch provider is based in Ottobrunn, Germany.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice without additional context or details provided in the news snippet.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA published a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication commemorates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's parabolic flight campaign. The study found that lasers can propel graphene aerogels with greater force in space-like conditions compared to Earth.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that integrates multiple functions traditionally handled by separate components. This innovation could revolutionize space electronics by reducing size, weight, and power consumption for spacecraft systems.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team confirmed the successful recovery of the spacecraft.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), focusing on planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator (LSS) chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is expected to launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. The visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on 30 March 2026, mandating its suppliers to use the new costing software for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Starfish Closes $100M+ Series B
summary: Starfish Space has raised over $100 million in a Series B funding round led by Point72 Ventures, Activate Capital, and Shield Capital. This investment will support the company's expansion in the in-space servicing sector.

title: The State of EO 2026
summary: The Earth Observation (EO) industry is experiencing growth as more entities seek to deploy their own satellite capabilities. The trend is highlighted in 'The State of EO 2026' report.

title: How Earthlings Got Views of the Lunar Fly By
summary: Redwire provided 11 cameras for the Orion capsule, enabling live views of the lunar flyby during the Artemis mission. These cameras were installed both inside and outside the spacecraft.

title: The State of Satcom 2026
summary: SpaceX and Amazon are disrupting the satcom market, forcing traditional players and startups to pivot and find innovative ways to compete.

title: Artemis II Supplier Series: Orion’s Windows
summary: The article discusses the importance of Orion's windows for astronauts on the Artemis II mission, highlighting their significance in space exploration. It is part of a series focusing on suppliers for the Artemis II mission.

title: White House Requests $18.8B FY27 Budget for NASA
summary: The White House has proposed a $18.8 billion budget for NASA in fiscal year 2027, maintaining the same funding level as the 2026 request but including cuts to science and STEM missions.

title: Insurance is Commercial Space Nuclear’s Biggest Headache
summary: Insurance is identified as the biggest challenge for commercial space nuclear missions, with experts deeming it a 'show stopper.' The issue was highlighted during an industry seminar.

title: Charts Defining the Space Industry in Q1 2026
summary: The article discusses key trends and statistics defining the space industry in Q1 2026, with a focus on the resurgence of Earth Observation (EO) applications. It highlights the growing importance of EO in the commercial space sector.

title: CLPS Companies Excited For NASA’s ‘Opportunity Bomb’ Lunar Plan
summary: NASA announced a plan for a lunar base supplied through monthly uncrewed lunar landings starting next year. CLPS companies are highly enthusiastic about this new opportunity.

title: Phantom Space Acquires Thermal Management Technologies
summary: Phantom Space has acquired Thermal Management Technologies, a company specializing in advanced satellite thermal components for in-space applications. This acquisition aims to enhance Phantom Space's capabilities in satellite thermal management systems.

title: Artemis II Crew Travels Farther from Earth Than Apollo 13 in a New Record
summary: The Artemis II crew has traveled farther from Earth than any previous human mission, surpassing the Apollo 13 record. This milestone marks a significant achievement for NASA's Artemis program.

title: Trump 2027 Budget Request Targets Cuts for NASA, Boost for Golden Dome
summary: The White House's fiscal 2027 budget proposal includes significant cuts to NASA funding while fully funding the Golden Dome missile defense initiative. This budget request reflects a shift toward increased defense spending.

title: How Iceye Satellites Track Russian Vessels, Illegal Shipping in the Arctic
summary: Iceye satellites are used to monitor Russian vessels and illegal shipping activities in the Arctic. The technology aids in tracking movements even in challenging conditions like dark nights and cloud cover.

title: L3Harris and Comtech Release Multi-Orbit Tactical Satcom Modem
summary: L3Harris and Comtech have jointly released a new multi-orbit tactical satcom embeddable modem designed for specific L3Harris terminals. The C2 modem, announced on March 31, enhances satellite communication capabilities.

title: Live in the Booth: Matt Desch Talks Iridium NTN Direct and Alt PNT Advances
summary: Iridium is launching Iridium NTN Direct, which CEO Matt Desch claims will be the first standardized global narrow-band satellite IoT service. The service aims to advance alternative PNT (Positioning, Navigation, and Timing) capabilities.

title: Redwire Wins European Quantum Satellite Contract
summary: Redwire has secured a contract to develop a quantum-secure satellite for Europe under ESA's Quantum Key Distribution Satellite (QKDSat) program.

title: African Market for Satellite Services Offers Pent Up Demand
summary: Africa's large landmass of 11.7 million square miles presents challenges for satellite services, but the market is changing as of September 2025. The region shows pent-up demand for satellite services.

title: Amazon in Talks to Buy Globalstar, According to FT Report
summary: Amazon is reportedly in discussions to acquire Globalstar, according to a Financial Times report. The potential deal could expand Amazon's satellite and communications capabilities.

title: Gravitics Receives Strategic Funding Increase From SpaceWERX
summary: Gravitics secured a Strategic Funding Increase (STRATFI) contract from SpaceWERX to accelerate the development of its Orbital Carrier architecture. This funding aims to support the U.S. Space Force's innovation initiatives.

title: Astroscale and Exotrail to Co-Develop Servicing Mission
summary: Astroscale and Exotrail will collaborate to develop a satellite de-orbiting mission, combining Japanese and French expertise in on-orbit servicing. The initiative aims to enhance capabilities for safely removing satellites from orbit.
